title: Biology Paper 6 Alternative to Practical - Mark Scheme

questions.question: A student investigated the response of some small animals called woodlice to various stimuli. A circular container with a lid called a 'choice chamber' was used. This had four distinct areas: dry and light, damp and light, dry and dark, and damp and dark.

parts.question: Count the numbers of woodlice in each area of choice chamber C and record them in the table.
Calculate the mean number of woodlice in each of the four areas of the choice chamber to the nearest whole number. Record these results in the table.
Construct a bar chart on the grid provided to show the mean number of woodlice (to the nearest whole number) found in each area of the choice chamber.
parts.answer: both axes fully labelled: mean number of woodlice / correct conditions with labels central to bars; linear scale + value at origin on mean number axis + at least half of each axis used in both directions; 4 plots correct; bars of equal width with ruled lines

parts.question: Using the data in the table and your bar chart, state which area of the choice chamber the woodlice preferred.
Suggest where the student should have placed the 10 woodlice when she first put them into the choice chamber. Explain your answer.
parts.answer: placed in the centre / random; to avoid bias

parts.question: Suggest two ways in which the results of this investigation could be made more reliable.
parts.answer: more repeats; leave longer than 20 minutes / give time to detect surroundings; more woodlice / only use each woodlouse once / use different woodlice; same type / species of woodlouse

parts.question: Another student says that it would be a good idea to make some holes in the lid of the choice chamber. Suggest one advantage and one disadvantage of doing this.
parts.answer: advantage: provide air / oxygen / able to add more woodlice; disadvantage: affects moisture or humidity / dries out / woodlice can escape

questions.question: The photograph shows a mammalian organ.

parts.question: Identify the organ labelled D.
Identify the structure labelled E.
In the space below, make a large drawing of structures D and E as they appear in the photograph. Do not include the surface detail of structure D in your drawing.
parts.answer: 1 clear and clean outlines of lung drawn with a sharp pencil + no shading; 2 trachea present + joins lung about 1/3 from top + extends beyond top of lung; 3 detail on trachea + ridged sides; 4 minimum size; 5 height greater than width + shape detail

parts.question: On the photograph, draw a line between F and G. Measure and record this length.
On your drawing, draw a line at the same location as the line F–G. Measure and record the length of this line.
parts.answer: line in matching place; correct measurement

parts.question: Use your measurements in (c)(i) and (c)(ii) to calculate the magnification of your drawing compared to the photograph. Give your answer to one decimal place.
parts.answer: measurement in (c)(ii) / measurement in (c)(i); correct answer rounded to 1dp

parts.question: The photomicrographs show the detail of the same organ from a smoker and a non-smoker. Both photomicrographs are to the same scale. Complete the table to identify two differences between the two photomicrographs.
parts.answer: smoker: large / many alveoli / few or no small blood vessels; non-smoker: small / few alveoli / more / larger blood vessels

questions.question: A student investigated the effect of exercise on her pulse rate using the Step Test.

parts.question: Complete the table by calculating the total number of heart beats for the 3 minutes after the step exercise.
The resting pulse rate of the student before exercising was 16 heart beats in 15 seconds. Calculate the number of heart beats in a minute for this resting pulse rate.
Use your answer to (a)(ii) and the data in the table to state the effect of exercise on the pulse rate of this student.
The fitness rating of an individual can be calculated using the formula shown. Use the information given above to calculate the student's fitness and to determine her fitness rating.
Describe the differences in pulse rate between the unfit student and the fit student as shown in the graph.
parts.answer: for fit student: 1 lower pulse rate throughout / overall; 2 lower resting pulse rate / pulse rate at start or 0–2 minutes or 52 (fit) v 76 (unfit); 3 pulse rate increases more slowly during exercise; 4 at peak / at 6 minutes, pulse rate is lower or 104 (fit) v 140 (unfit); 5 pulse rate returns to (near) resting / stable more quickly; max 3 ORA throughout

parts.question: Use the graph to calculate the difference in pulse rate between the unfit student and the fit student 6 minutes after starting to exercise.
parts.answer: two correct values for unfit (112 ± 1) and fit student (66 ± 1); correct calculation with units (beats per minute or bpm)
